title: Smoky Roasted Egyptian Malt Hash
description: A malty, fluffy, lazy-sunday Egyptian dish featuring sake-cooking, double-fry KFC technique, and Ayurvedic sattvic-friendly ingredients, served in a wok.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in the wok over medium-high heat.
Add 1 cup of sake and cook until the liquid has almost completely evaporated, stirring occasionally.
Add 2 cups of short-grain Egyptian rice and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add 1 cup of malted barley flour and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Transfer the wok to the preheated oven and roast at 400°F for 20 minutes.
While the rice is roasting, heat 1 tablespoon of ghee in a small skillet over low heat.
Add 1/4 cup of chopped fresh parsley and cook until fragrant.
Remove the wok from the oven and fluff the rice with a spatula.
Add the cooked parsley mixture and stir to combine.
Double-fry the remaining 1 cup of short-grain Egyptian rice according to the KFC double-fry technique.
Combine the roasted rice and double-fried rice in a large bowl and stir to combine.
Season with cumin, cayenne pepper, and flake salt to taste.
